This module generates a DEF file from the symbols in
an MSVC-compiled DLL import library.  It correctly discriminates between
data and functions.  The data is collected from the output of the program
nm(1).
Usage:
    python lib2def.py [libname.lib] [output.def]
    python lib2def.py [libname.lib] > output.def
libname.lib defaults to python<py_ver>.lib and output.def defaults to stdout
Author: Robert Kern <kernr@mail.ncifcrf.gov>
Last Update: April 30, 1999
0.1az
%d%d
-CszrLIBRARY         python%s.dll
;CODE           PRELOAD MOVEABLE DISCARDABLE
;DATA           PRELOAD SINGLE
EXPORTS
^(.*) in python%s\.dllz
^_imp__(.*) in python%s\.dllc
zBParses the command-line arguments.
libfile, deffile = parse_cmd()
.libr
.defz4I'm assuming that your first argument is the libraryz
and the second is the DEF file.
python%s.lib)
argv
print
py_ver)
libfile
deffile
=/usr/lib64/python3.9/site-packages/numpy/distutils/lib2def.py
	parse_cmd&
zUReturns the output of nm_cmd via a pipe.
nm_output = getnm(nm_cmd = 'nm -Cs py_lib')T)
shell
stdout
stderrZ

failed to run "%s": "%s"

	nm_outputZ

getnm>
Returns a tuple of lists: dlist for the list of data
symbols and flist for the list of function symbols.
dlist, flist = parse_nm(nm_output)Nr
init)

sort)

dlistr
parse_nmJ
zoOutputs the final DEF file to a file defaulting to stdout.
output_def(dlist, flist, header, file = sys.stdout)z		%s DATA
